title: excoecaria diversifolia
intro: Excoecaria diversifolia, also known as the poison tree or Australian milk tree, is a plant species native to Australia, Asia, and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its toxic milky sap and distinctive leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria diversifolia include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although caution is advised due to its toxic sap.
